result.stdout:
Description:
  Metreja - .NET Call-Path Profiler CLI

Usage:
  metreja [command] [options]

Options:
  --debug         Enable debug logging to stderr
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  init                           Initialize a new profiling session
  add                            Add include or exclude filter rules
  remove                         Remove include or exclude filter rules
  clear-filters                  Clear all filter rules from a session
  set                            Set session configuration values
  validate                       Validate session configuration
  generate-env                   Generate environment variable script for profiling
  run <exe-path> <extra-args>    Launch an executable with profiler environment variables attached
  clear                          Delete profiling session(s)
  analyze-diff <base> <compare>  Compare two NDJSON profiling outputs
  hotspots <file>                Show per-method timing hotspots with self time
  calltree <file>                Show the call tree for a specific method invocation
  callers <file>                 Show which methods call a specific method
  memory <file>                  Show GC summary and allocation hotspots by class
  summary <file>                 Show trace overview: events, threads, methods, duration
  exceptions <file>              Rank exception types by frequency with throw-site methods
  timeline <file>                Chronological event listing with filtering
  threads <file>                 Per-thread breakdown: call counts, timing, activity windows
  trend <file>                   Method performance trend across periodic stats flushes
  check <base> <compare>         CI regression gate: compare traces, exit non-zero on regression
  list                           List existing profiling sessions
  merge <files>                  Combine multiple trace files into one sorted by timestamp
  export <file>                  Convert traces to external formats (speedscope, csv)
  report                         Report an issue to the GitHub repository
  flush                          Trigger manual stats flush on a running profiled process

commands.payload:
Description:
  Metreja - .NET Call-Path Profiler CLI

Usage:
  metreja [command] [options]

Options:
  --debug         Enable debug logging to stderr
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  init                           Initialize a new profiling session
  add                            Add include or exclude filter rules
  remove                         Remove include or exclude filter rules
  clear-filters                  Clear all filter rules from a session
  set                            Set session configuration values
  validate                       Validate session configuration
  generate-env                   Generate environment variable script for profiling
  run <exe-path> <extra-args>    Launch an executable with profiler environment variables attached
  clear                          Delete profiling session(s)
  analyze-diff <base> <compare>  Compare two NDJSON profiling outputs
  hotspots <file>                Show per-method timing hotspots with self time
  calltree <file>                Show the call tree for a specific method invocation
  callers <file>                 Show which methods call a specific method
  memory <file>                  Show GC summary and allocation hotspots by class
  summary <file>                 Show trace overview: events, threads, methods, duration
  exceptions <file>              Rank exception types by frequency with throw-site methods
  timeline <file>                Chronological event listing with filtering
  threads <file>                 Per-thread breakdown: call counts, timing, activity windows
  trend <file>                   Method performance trend across periodic stats flushes
  check <base> <compare>         CI regression gate: compare traces, exit non-zero on regression
  list                           List existing profiling sessions
  merge <files>                  Combine multiple trace files into one sorted by timestamp
  export <file>                  Convert traces to external formats (speedscope, csv)
  report                         Report an issue to the GitHub repository
  flush                          Trigger manual stats flush on a running profiled process

result.stdout:
Description:
  Add an exclude filter rule

Usage:
  metreja add exclude [options]

Options:
  -s, --session <session> (REQUIRED)  Session ID
  --assembly <assembly>               Assembly name pattern(s)
  --namespace <namespace>             Namespace pattern(s)
  --class <class>                     Class name pattern(s)
  --method <method>                   Method name pattern(s)
  -?, -h, --help                      Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Compare two NDJSON profiling outputs

Usage:
  metreja analyze-diff <base> <compare> [options]

Arguments:
  <base>     Base NDJSON file path
  <compare>  Comparison NDJSON file path

Options:
  --format <json|text>  Output format: text or json [default: text]
  -?, -h, --help        Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Show which methods call a specific method

Usage:
  metreja callers <file> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>  NDJSON trace file path

Options:
  --method <method> (REQUIRED)  Method name or pattern to match
  --top <top>                   Number of callers to show [default: 20]
  --format <json|text>          Output format: text or json [default: text]
  -?, -h, --help                Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Show the call tree for a specific method invocation

Usage:
  metreja calltree <file> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>  NDJSON trace file path

Options:
  --method <method> (REQUIRED)  Method name or pattern to match
  --tid <tid>                   Filter by thread ID
  --occurrence <occurrence>     Which occurrence to show (1 = slowest) [default: 1]
  --format <json|text>          Output format: text or json [default: text]
  -?, -h, --help                Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  CI regression gate: compare traces, exit non-zero on regression

Usage:
  metreja check <base> <compare> [options]

Arguments:
  <base>     Base NDJSON trace file path
  <compare>  Compare NDJSON trace file path

Options:
  --threshold <threshold>  Regression threshold percentage [default: 10]
  --format <json|text>     Output format: text or json [default: text]
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate environment variable script for profiling

Usage:
  metreja generate-env [options]

Options:
  -s, --session <session> (REQUIRED)  Session ID
  --format <format>                   Output format: batch, powershell, or shell [default: shell]
  --force                             Generate script even if profiler DLL is not found
  -?, -h, --help                      Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Show per-method timing hotspots with self time

Usage:
  metreja hotspots <file> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>  NDJSON trace file path

Options:
  --top <top>           Number of methods to show [default: 20]
  --min-ms <min-ms>     Minimum time threshold in milliseconds [default: 0]
  --sort <sort>         Sort by: self, inclusive, calls, or allocs [default: self]
  --filter <filter>     Include only methods matching pattern(s) (method, class, or namespace)
  --format <json|text>  Output format: text or json [default: text]
  -?, -h, --help        Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

  --filter <filter>     Include only methods matching pattern(s) (method, class, or namespace)
  --format <json|text>  Output format: text or json [default: text]
  -?, -h, --help        Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Launch an executable with profiler environment variables attached

Usage:
  metreja run <exe-path> [<extra-args>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <exe-path>    Path to the executable to profile
  <extra-args>  Additional arguments passed to the executable

Options:
  -s, --session <session> (REQUIRED)  Session ID
  --detach                            Launch and return immediately (for GUI/long-running apps)
  -?, -h, --help                      Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Set session configuration values

Usage:
  metreja set [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  metadata <scenario>             Set metadata values
  output <path>                   Set output path
  max-events <value>              Set maximum event count
  compute-deltas <value>          Enable or disable delta timing
  events <events>                 Set enabled event types
  stats-flush-interval <seconds>  Set periodic stats flush interval

  output <path>                   Set output path
  max-events <value>              Set maximum event count
  compute-deltas <value>          Enable or disable delta timing
  events <events>                 Set enabled event types
  stats-flush-interval <seconds>  Set periodic stats flush interval

result.stdout:
Description:
  Chronological event listing with filtering

Usage:
  metreja timeline <file> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>  NDJSON trace file path

Options:
  --tid <tid>                Filter by thread ID
  --event-type <event-type>  Filter by event type
  --method <method>          Filter by method pattern
  --top <top>                Maximum events to show [default: 100]
  --format <json|text>       Output format: text or json [default: text]
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Chronological event listing with filtering

Usage:
  metreja timeline <file> [options]

Arguments:
  <file>  NDJSON trace file path

Options:
  --tid <tid>                Filter by thread ID
  --event-type <event-type>  Filter by event type
  --method <method>          Filter by method pattern
  --top <top>                Maximum events to show [default: 100]
  --format <json|text>       Output format: text or json [default: text]
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information
